title: The Gang Solves the Bathroom Problem

jokes.text: I hope he plays 'The Piña Colada Song,' man... Yeah, no, I know the song, I know the song, but it's sung by a man named Rupert Holmes.

dialogue.line: Wait a second, Dee. So now you're okay with Mac using your bathroom?

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah. As long as the majority of the people aren't taking a shit in my bathroom, sure.

jokes.text: I like mine with lettuce and tomato. ♪ Heinz 57 and French fried potatoes. ♪ But that's just me, you know?
jokes.setup: Dennis making an analogy about personal preferences
jokes.punchline: References 'Cheeseburger in Paradise' while discussing majority rule
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Buffett theme while making political argument

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I like mine with lettuce and tomato. ♪ Heinz 57 and French fried potatoes. ♪ But that's just me, you know?

jokes.text: Hawaiians are savages.
jokes.setup: Discussion about pineapple on pizza
jokes.punchline: Frank's casual racist generalization
jokes.explanation: Typical Frank casual bigotry - effective character moment but predictable

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Hawaiians are savages.

jokes.text: And societies are judged on how they treat their most vulnerable. See, we have to give voices to those who have no voice.
jokes.setup: Mac making a passionate progressive argument
jokes.punchline: His shallow understanding of progressive rhetoric
jokes.explanation: Mac's performative progressiveness without real understanding

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: And societies are judged on how they treat their most vulnerable. See, we have to give voices to those who have no voice.

jokes.text: Shut up, Dee! I'm talking! Women are always... Goddamn it.
jokes.setup: Mac making arguments about protecting the marginalized
jokes.punchline: Immediately silences and generalizes about women
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction - Mac undermining his own progressive argument immediately

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Shut up, Dee! I'm talking! Women are always... Goddamn it.

jokes.text: We need to change our attitudes. We need to change our latitudes.
jokes.setup: Mac's continued progressive speech
jokes.punchline: Sneaks in Buffett reference while making serious point
jokes.explanation: Clever Buffett reference worked into progressive speech

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We need to change our attitudes. We need to change our latitudes.

jokes.text: Wait, wait, wait! I just looked up the demographics of Philly. It says here that this city is 54% women and 68% Christian.
jokes.setup: Debate about who counts as minorities
jokes.punchline: Charlie suddenly producing demographic data
jokes.explanation: Good twist with Charlie suddenly being prepared with actual data

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait! I just looked up the demographics of Philly. It says here that this city is 54% women and 68% Christian.

jokes.text: It says here that this city is 54% women and 68% Christian... which means that Mac and Dee are in the majority... which means white men, like me and Dennis, are in the minority.
jokes.setup: Charlie doing demographic research
jokes.punchline: Statistics completely reverse their minority/majority arguments
jokes.explanation: Excellent reversal - Charlie's research destroys everyone's self-serving arguments

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It says here that this city is 54% women and 68% Christian... which means that Mac and Dee are in the majority... which means white men, like me and Dennis, are in the minority.

jokes.text: I don't know. They're at war.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's more gay than Catholic
jokes.punchline: Describes his internal identity conflict as warfare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac character moment - his identity crisis as internal warfare

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't know. They're at war.

jokes.text: You are white. And I'm a...? Man. And that would make me...? In the minority. Which means I go towards...? Us.
jokes.setup: Frank walking Dennis through demographic logic
jokes.punchline: Dennis's slow realization he benefits from minority status
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dennis's opportunistic calculation

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: You are white. And I'm a...? Man. And that would make me...? In the minority. Which means I go towards...? Us.

jokes.text: Finally, a win for straight white men, right? Yeah! Finally, something for us. Something for the guys.
jokes.setup: They've manipulated minority status for themselves
jokes.punchline: Celebrating 'finally' getting something as historically privileged group
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic reversal - straight white men celebrating minority status

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Finally, a win for straight white men, right?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah! Finally, something for us. Something for the guys.

jokes.text: ♪ Aruba, Jamaica... ♪ That's Beach Boys. It is.
jokes.setup: Charlie asked to name Buffett songs
jokes.punchline: Immediately singing Beach Boys and being corrected
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier musical confusion - Charlie proving he doesn't know Buffett

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: ♪ Aruba, Jamaica... ♪

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's Beach Boys.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: It is.

jokes.text: You used Buffett's words to promote discrimination, dude. It's not cool.
jokes.setup: Mac refusing to go to the concert
jokes.punchline: Claims moral high ground about using Buffett for discrimination
jokes.explanation: Mac's selective moral outrage about misusing Buffett lyrics

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You used Buffett's words to promote discrimination, dude. It's not cool.

jokes.text: ♪ Aruba, Jamaica... ♪ That's Beach Boys. It is. I don't know the songs.
jokes.setup: Testing Charlie's Buffett knowledge
jokes.punchline: Confirms Charlie knows zero Buffett songs despite 20 years of concerts
jokes.explanation: Good callback confirming Charlie's complete musical ignorance

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: ♪ Aruba, Jamaica... ♪ That's Beach Boys. It is. I don't know the songs.

jokes.text: It's about a guy trying to cheat on his wife. Don't tell me that, man. It's, like, such a happy song.
jokes.setup: Mac reveals the dark lyrics of the Piña Colada song
jokes.punchline: Dennis's innocent view being shattered
jokes.explanation: Good dark revelation about the supposedly innocent song

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's about a guy trying to cheat on his wife.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Don't tell me that, man. It's, like, such a happy song.

jokes.text: As a straight man who poops transgender... You got to just let that go. I'll probably stop doing that. It's not a thing.
jokes.setup: Charlie still trying to define his bathroom identity
jokes.punchline: Group intervention to stop his weird self-categorization
jokes.explanation: Good callback with group finally shutting down Charlie's weird logic

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: As a straight man who poops transgender...
You got to just let that go.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: I'll probably stop doing that.
It's not a thing.

jokes.text: In there, we're all just disgusting, vulnerable, ashamed. Like a bunch of filthy animals dumping in a shithouse.
jokes.setup: Charlie making a philosophical point about bathroom equality
jokes.punchline: His characteristically crude and honest description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie wisdom - profound truth delivered in the crudest possible way

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: In there, we're all just disgusting, vulnerable, ashamed. Like a bunch of filthy animals dumping in a shithouse.

jokes.text: Dee... we were just talking, and you just barged right in... Shut the fuck up. Like, interrupting and talking nonsense.
jokes.setup: Dee tries to contribute to the philosophical discussion
jokes.punchline: Immediate dismissal and hostility despite talk of treating people well
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction - preaching kindness while being cruel to Dee

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dee... we were just talking, and you just barged right in... Shut the fuck up. Like, interrupting and talking nonsense.

jokes.text: Visual: Final bathroom signs that are completely illegible scribbles
jokes.setup: Hours of debate about proper bathroom signage
jokes.punchline: Final solution is incomprehensible scrawl that solves nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual punchline - their 'solution' is completely useless gibberish

jokes.text: Everyone singing Piña Colada song with wrong lyrics as they go to concert
jokes.setup: Episode opened with confusion about this song
jokes.punchline: They're all singing it wrong together, proving Charlie was right to be confused
jokes.explanation: Nice bookend callback showing they're all equally ignorant about the songs

dialogue.line: Everyone singing Piña Colada song with wrong lyrics as they go to concert

tmdb_overview: Jimmy Buffett is in town and the positive vibes are alive at the bar. But when Mac comes out of the women's bathroom, no one can leave until the bathroom situation is solved.
